At inference time, SSA scores chunks via attention between the current query and the small set of gist tokens, selectively unfolding the top-k chunks by reintroducing their corresponding raw tokens. Since the query is scored only against the gist tokens, we avoid the memory-bandwidth cost associated with naive scoring against the full KV cache, without requiring the auxiliary KV cache approach used by sparse attention methods. On LongBench, SSA consistently outperforms compression and inference-time sparse-attention baselines under the same compression ratio. More strikingly, in retrieval-augmented generation, SSA can even outperform full attention after continued pretraining by over 5.7 points. We attribute this to the ability of SSA's selective unfolding, which concentrates attention on the query-relevant chunks and effectively filters out noise. SSA further extends to a hierarchical gist-of-gist variant (H-SSA) that achieves log-linear decoding complexity while maintaining or improving accuracy at high compression ratios up to 32x. The code is available at https://github.com/yuzhenmao/simplified-sparse-attention/.

ru.title: Умное сжатие контекста без перестройки модели
ru.desc: В статье представлен метод Simplified Sparse Attention (SSA), который снижает вычислительные затраты при обработке длинных контекстов без изменения архитектуры модели. Ключевая идея заключается в добавлении специальных gist-токенов во время дообучения, которые сжимают информацию о каждом блоке текста с использованием маски внимания. Во время инференса модель оценивает релевантность блоков, сравнивая текущий запрос только с gist-токенами, а затем выбирает топ-k наиболее важные блоки для развёртывания. Иерархическое расширение H-SSA достигает логарифмической сложности декодирования при сохранении качества с коэффициентом сжатия до 32x.

en.title: Efficient Long-Context Inference with Simplified Sparse Attention
en.desc: Simplified Sparse Attention (SSA) is a new method that improves the efficiency of long-context inference in language models without changing their architecture. It uses gist tokens during pretraining to help the model focus on important information, allowing it to score relevant chunks of data more effectively at inference time. By only attending to these gist tokens, SSA reduces memory and computational costs compared to traditional methods. The results show that SSA outperforms existing sparse attention techniques and even full attention in certain tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness in managing context efficiently.

zh.title: 简化稀疏注意力：高效的长上下文推理新方法
zh.desc: 简化稀疏注意力（SSA）通过在预训练期间使用要点令牌的注意力掩码，降低了长上下文推理的成本。SSA不需要对模型架构进行修改，采用了一种更简单的稀疏注意力方法。它通过将重要信息打包到要点令牌中，优化了标准的下一个令牌损失，并在推理时根据要点令牌选择相关的上下文块。实验结果表明，SSA在长上下文推理中表现优于传统的稀疏注意力方法，且在高压缩比下仍能保持或提高准确性。

papers.title: MemoBench: Benchmarking World Modeling in Dynamically Changing Environments
papers.abstract: Abstract MemoBench presents a diagnostic benchmark for evaluating video generation models' memory consistency in dynamically changing environments where objects disappear and reappear in updated states.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Video generation models aspire to simulate dynamic environments, and several benchmarks now evaluate memory consistency across frames. However, most assess consistency only while the target remains in view, and the few that force objects out of view evaluate static scenes where nothing changes during occlusion. To bridge this gap, we introduce MemoBench, a diagnostic benchmark built around the disappear-and-reappear paradigm in dynamically changing environments: a target object undergoes a physical process, disappears from view, and must be correctly recovered in its updated state upon reappearance. We curate 360 ground-truth clips spanning synthetic and real-world scenes, and design an evaluation suite combining automated metrics with VQA-based assessment across four diagnostic pillars. Evaluation of eight state-of-the-art models reveals key insights and open challenges regarding memory consistency under the disappear-and-reappear paradigm.

ru.title: Оценка памяти видеомоделей через исчезновение и переоформление объектов
ru.desc: MemoBench представляет диагностический бенчмарк для оценки способности моделей генерации видео правильно запоминать состояние объектов в динамически изменяющихся сценах. Основной сценарий оценки — когда объект претерпевает физические изменения, исчезает из кадра и должен быть восстановлен в обновлённом состоянии при его повторном появлении. Бенчмарк включает 360 эталонных видеоклипов из синтетических и реальных сцен с комбинированной системой метрик оценки и VQA-подходом. Тестирование восьми передовых моделей выявило ключевые проблемы в обеспечении семантической согласованности памяти объектов при сценарии исчезновения и появления.

en.title: Evaluating Memory Consistency in Dynamic Video Generation
en.desc: MemoBench is a new benchmark designed to test how well video generation models maintain memory consistency when objects disappear and reappear in dynamic environments. Unlike previous benchmarks that only check consistency when objects are visible, MemoBench evaluates models in scenarios where objects are occluded and must be accurately reconstructed upon reappearance. The benchmark includes 360 ground-truth video clips from both synthetic and real-world settings, and it employs a mix of automated metrics and visual question answering (VQA) assessments. By analyzing eight advanced models, MemoBench highlights important insights and challenges in achieving reliable memory consistency in video generation.

zh.title: 动态环境中的内存一致性评估基准
zh.desc: MemoBench是一个用于评估视频生成模型在动态环境中内存一致性的基准测试工具。该基准测试关注对象在消失和重新出现的过程中，如何保持生成视频的连贯性。我们设计了360个真实和合成场景的基准片段，并结合自动化指标和基于视频问答的评估方法进行测试。通过对八个最先进模型的评估，我们揭示了内存一致性方面的关键见解和挑战。

papers.title: To Run or Not to Run: Analyzing the Cost-Effectiveness of Code Execution in LLM-Based Program Repair
papers.abstract: Abstract LLM-based program repair agents frequently use execution-based testing but show inconsistent efficiency, with execution costs outweighing benefits in many cases.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct LLM-based agents for program repair are increasingly built on a "generate-run-revise" paradigm, iteratively executing tests to evaluate and refine patches. This execution-based approach has become standard practice in state-of-the-art systems. However, executions can be time-consuming and expensive, yet their impact on these agents remains underexplored. In this paper, we conduct a two-stage empirical study over execution behavior in LLM-based program repair. To characterize execution behavior at scale, we first analyze 7,745 agent traces from SWE-bench leaderboard submissions. Second, we evaluate 3,000 end-to-end repair attempts across 200 SWE-bench instances and three agents (Claude Code, Codex, and the open-source OpenCode) under four execution paradigms, which allows for a fine-grained comparison of performance and cost. Our analysis reveals three key observations: (1) Code execution is used across all agents and models analyzed, with an average of 8.8 test runs per task. Execution behavior varies substantially across agents and models, with frequency ranging from 2 to 19 per task, and late-stage executions consistently achieve higher success rates than early-stage ones. (2) Execution restrictions have little effect on repair success: on commercial agents with SOTA models the resolve-rate gap between Prohibited and Unrestricted is only 1.25 percentage points and not statistically significant, while Prohibited saves substantial token and wall-clock cost. (3) Execution benefit is concentrated rather than uniform. These patterns suggest that current agents apply execution indiscriminately, paying its cost on instances where it provides little benefit. Execution, therefore, should be treated as a resource with an explicit cost-benefit tradeoff, not a default capability.

ru.title: Выполнение кода в агентах восстановления программ: затраты превышают выгоды
ru.desc: В работе проводится эмпирическое исследование использования выполнения кода в агентах на основе больших языковых моделей для автоматического исправления программ. Авторы анализируют 7,745 трассировок агентов и проводят 3,000 экспериментов с тремя различными агентами, выявляя, что исполнение тестов используется избыточно без учета его стоимости. Исследование показывает, что ограничение выполнения кода незначительно влияет на успешность исправления, снижая расходы на вычисления и токены, но существующие агенты применяют выполнение индискриминантно. Основной вывод состоит в том, что выполнение кода следует рассматривать как ресурс с явным соотношением затрат и выгод, а не как встроенную возможность.

en.title: Optimize Execution: A Cost-Benefit Approach to Program Repair
en.desc: This paper investigates the efficiency of LLM-based program repair agents that utilize execution-based testing. It highlights that while these agents follow a 'generate-run-revise' approach, the costs of execution often outweigh the benefits, leading to inconsistent performance. Through a detailed empirical study, the authors analyze execution behavior across multiple agents and tasks, revealing that execution frequency and success rates vary significantly. The findings suggest that execution should be strategically managed as a resource, emphasizing a cost-benefit analysis rather than treating it as a standard practice.

zh.title: 执行测试：资源与收益的权衡
zh.desc: 本文探讨了基于大型语言模型（LLM）的程序修复代理在执行测试时的效率问题。研究发现，尽管执行测试是当前主流的修复方法，但其成本往往超过收益，尤其是在某些情况下。通过对7745个代理跟踪和3000个修复尝试的分析，揭示了执行行为的多样性和执行限制对修复成功率的影响微乎其微。最后，作者建议将执行视为一种资源，需在成本和收益之间进行权衡，而非默认的能力。

papers.title: The Galaxy's Guide to the Tokenizer: A Benchmark for Scientific Foundation Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Four tokenization methods for astronomical images show distinct strengths in reconstruction quality, physical property prediction, and morphological preservation, with no single approach excelling across all tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Tokenization is central to adapting scientific data for transformer-based foundation models, yet its impact on learned representations remains poorly understood. We compare four tokenization strategies, Affine, AIM, JetFormer, and VQ-VAE, within a unified transformer framework for astronomical imaging. Using 640,000 galaxy images from the DESI Legacy Survey and a shared AstroPT backbone, we evaluate each method on reconstruction fidelity and prediction of physical properties. Our results reveal trade-offs across approaches. The flow-based JetFormer achieves higher reconstruction quality, while VQ-VAE yields strong probe performance for galaxy physical properties. Affine and AIM better preserve localized morphological information. We find that reconstruction and representation quality are decoupled, and no single method consistently performs best across the tasks considered here. By grounding our evaluation in independently measured physical quantities, we hope this study serves to highlight the potential of scientific data as a basis for constructing interpretable benchmarks for foundation models.

ru.title: Компромиссы в выборе токенизации для фундаментальных моделей астрономических данных
ru.desc: Исследование сравнивает четыре метода токенизации (Affine, AIM, JetFormer и VQ-VAE) для астрономических изображений галактик в рамках трансформер-архитектуры. Авторы оценивают каждый подход на наборе из 640,000 изображений по качеству реконструкции и предсказанию физических свойств объектов. Результаты показывают, что различные методы токенизации имеют разные сильные стороны: JetFormer лучше восстанавливает изображения, VQ-VAE эффективнее для предсказания физических характеристик, а Affine и AIM лучше сохраняют локальную морфологическую информацию. Исследование демонстрирует, что качество реконструкции и качество представлений независимы друг от друга, и ни один метод не превосходит остальные по всем метрикам одновременно.

en.title: Optimizing Tokenization for Astronomical Image Analysis
en.desc: This paper investigates four different tokenization methods for processing astronomical images to improve the performance of transformer models. The methods compared are Affine, AIM, JetFormer, and VQ-VAE, each showing unique advantages in tasks like image reconstruction and predicting physical properties of galaxies. The study finds that while JetFormer excels in reconstruction quality, VQ-VAE is better for predicting physical attributes, and Affine and AIM are superior in maintaining morphological details. Overall, the research highlights that no single tokenization method is optimal for all tasks, emphasizing the need for tailored approaches in scientific data applications.

zh.title: 探索天文图像标记化的最佳策略
zh.desc: 本文探讨了四种天文图像的标记化方法，包括Affine、AIM、JetFormer和VQ-VAE。这些方法在重建质量、物理属性预测和形态保持方面各有优劣，没有一种方法在所有任务中表现最佳。研究表明，重建质量和表示质量是解耦的，且不同方法在不同任务中表现出不同的权衡。通过使用独立测量的物理量进行评估，本文希望强调科学数据在构建可解释基准方面的潜力。

papers.title: CogniRoute: Learning to Route Social Evidence in Omni-Modal Models
papers.abstract: Abstract CogniRoute is a schema-guided Mixture-of-Experts framework for social video question answering that improves multimodal reasoning through cognitive schema factorization and route-aware reinforcement learning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Omni-modal models can ingest video, audio, and text, but unified access to multiple modalities does not guarantee that a model uses the right evidence. This gap is especially pronounced in social video question answering, where the answer may hinge on a gesture, vocal tone, temporal cue, or mismatch between what is said and what is visually expressed. We introduce CogniRoute, a schema-guided Mixture-of-Experts framework for social omni reasoning. CogniRoute uses a training-only cognitive schema that factorizes each example by cross-modal relation, reasoning demand, and temporal scope, and aligns global routing signatures with this structure during supervised fine-tuning. We further introduce route-aware reinforcement learning, which jointly optimizes token generation and expert allocation using rewards for answer correctness, modality-consistent reasoning, and cognitive temporal grounding. To support training and evaluation, we construct OmniSocialBench, a diagnostic social video QA resource with 118K structured training examples, grounded reasoning traces, schema labels, temporal evidence spans, and a manually verified evaluation split. CogniRoute achieves 59.38\% average accuracy on OmniSocialBench, improving over the strongest proprietary baseline by 15.33 percentage points and the strongest open-source omni baseline by 26.77 points, with the largest gains on questions requiring audio-visual coordination, conflict resolution, and temporally grounded social inference.

ru.title: Когнитивная маршрутизация экспертов для понимания социальных видео
ru.desc: CogniRoute — это схемно-ориентированная архитектура Mixture-of-Experts для ответов на вопросы о социальных видео, которая улучшает мультимодальное рассуждение через факторизацию когнитивной схемы. Фреймворк использует когнитивную схему для разложения примеров по кросс-модальным отношениям, требуемым рассуждениям и временным характеристикам, выравнивая глобальные сигналы маршрутизации с этой структурой. Авторы предложили route-aware обучение с подкреплением, которое совместно оптимизирует генерацию токенов и распределение экспертов через награды за корректность ответа и согласованность модальностей. Для поддержки тренировки и оценки создан набор данных OmniSocialBench с 118K структурированными примерами, включая аннотации схемы и временные диапазоны доказательств.

en.title: Enhancing Social Video QA with Cognitive Schema and Expert Routing
en.desc: CogniRoute is a new framework designed to enhance social video question answering by using a Mixture-of-Experts approach that incorporates cognitive schema factorization. It addresses the challenge of effectively utilizing different types of information, such as video, audio, and text, to provide accurate answers. The framework employs route-aware reinforcement learning to optimize both the generation of answers and the selection of expert models based on the context of the question. With the introduction of a comprehensive dataset called OmniSocialBench, CogniRoute demonstrates significant improvements in accuracy, particularly in complex scenarios requiring coordination of audio and visual cues.

zh.title: CogniRoute：提升社交视频问答的多模态推理能力
zh.desc: CogniRoute 是一个基于模式引导的专家混合框架，旨在提高社交视频问答中的多模态推理能力。它通过认知模式分解和路线感知强化学习来优化模型的推理过程。CogniRoute 采用训练专用的认知模式，将每个示例按跨模态关系、推理需求和时间范围进行分解，并在监督微调过程中与全局路由签名对齐。通过构建 OmniSocialBench 数据集，CogniRoute 在社交视频问答任务中实现了显著的准确率提升，特别是在需要音视频协调和时间推理的问题上。

papers.title: How Much Static Structure Do Code Agents Need? A Study of Deterministic Anchoring
papers.abstract: Abstract Static analysis annotations improve code agent navigation by providing deterministic structural anchors that enhance predictability and reduce variability in software exploration.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct LLM-based code agents navigate repositories through keyword search but miss the structural relationships, such as call graphs, inheritance hierarchies, and configuration dependencies, that define how software actually works. This makes agent navigation stochastic and difficult to reproduce across runs. We investigate whether lightweight static analysis can provide deterministic anchors for these agents: stable structural facts injected as plain-text comments that constrain probabilistic exploration and make navigation more predictable. Starting from a strong baseline, Codex from OpenAI, we systematically inject varying granularities of structural annotations and measure their effects on localization, trajectory behavior, and run-to-run stability. Our study identifies what we call the deterministic anchoring effect: static structure helps less by making agents "smarter" and more by making their navigation disciplined and reproducible. Three observations support this finding: (1) Anchoring works: lightweight call/inheritance topology improves function-level localization (+2.2pp Func@5) and shortens trajectories (-1.6 interaction rounds); (2) Anchoring is scale-sensitive: the optimal granularity and directionality depend on repository characteristics, where denser semantics show diminishing returns and hub-heavy projects benefit from inverse-only links that expose "who-calls-me" without forward edges; (3) Anchoring stabilizes: tags raise link-following rate from 0.15-0.18 to 0.21-0.24, roughly halve run-to-run variance, and improve single-run reliability (Pass@1 +3.4 pp) on medium-scale repositories, at the cost of roughly 10% more input tokens. These observations suggest practical guidelines: default to lightweight topology on medium projects, prune forward edges in large repositories, and reserve dense tags for implicit-dependency cases.

ru.title: Структурные якори для надёжной навигации кодовых агентов
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, как статический анализ в виде текстовых аннотаций помогает LLM-агентам более предсказуемо и воспроизводимо навигировать по кодовым репозиториям. Авторы вводят структурные метаданные (граф вызовов, иерархия наследования) прямо в исходный код и демонстрируют, что это снижает стохастичность поведения агента и повышает стабильность результатов между запусками. Ключевое открытие заключается в том, что аннотации работают не столько за счёт повышения интеллектуальности агента, сколько за счёт дисциплинирования его навигации и введения детерминированных якорей. Исследование предлагает практические рекомендации по оптимальной гранулярности аннотаций в зависимости от характеристик проекта.

en.title: Enhancing Code Navigation with Static Analysis Anchors
en.desc: This paper explores how static analysis annotations can enhance the navigation of code agents by providing clear structural anchors. These anchors help reduce randomness in the agents' exploration of software repositories, making their behavior more predictable and reproducible. The study shows that injecting lightweight structural annotations improves the agents' ability to locate functions and shortens their navigation paths. The findings suggest practical strategies for using these annotations effectively based on the characteristics of the codebase.

zh.title: 静态分析注释提升代码导航的可预测性
zh.desc: 本文探讨了静态分析注释如何改善代码代理的导航能力。通过提供确定性的结构锚点，这些注释增强了软件探索的可预测性，减少了变异性。研究表明，轻量级的静态分析可以为代码代理提供稳定的结构事实，从而使导航更加可重复。我们的实验结果显示，静态结构的引入不仅提高了代理的定位能力，还使其导航行为更加规范。

papers.title: Boundary-Aware Context Grounding for A Low-Channel EEG Agent
papers.abstract: Abstract NeuraDock Agent combines a deterministic EEG processing engine with a language model interface to ensure accurate, hardware-aware analysis while maintaining local data security.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language models (LLMs) can make scientific software easier to use. However, a general model does not automatically know which measurements a particular sensor can support, which algorithms are implemented in the current software, or which conclusions are justified by a computed result. These distinctions are especially important for low-channel electroencephalography (EEG), where sparse spatial coverage and variable signal quality make plausible but unsupported interpretations easy to produce. We present NeuraDock Agent, an open-source architecture that separates a deterministic local EEG engine from a hardware-aware language layer. The numerical engine parses recordings, performs quality control, executes reviewed spectral workflows, and writes machine-readable artifacts. The LLM receives only a compact, allowlisted summary and a versioned context pack. The context describes the seven-channel hardware, reviewed workflows, result fields, implementation boundaries, scientific limits, and reference cases. Raw EEG and dense per-sample arrays remain local   We evaluate the system at three levels. First, 12 recordings produced identical structured results over ten numerical repetitions, and a complete Rest/Task run produced identical result, report, and figure hashes over three repetitions. Second, request-capture and failure-injection experiments confirmed the tested data boundary and preservation of local artifacts under HTTP, malformed-output, and connection failures. Third, a boundary-awareness benchmark tested 36 ordinary and adversarial questions under four context ablations and two LLMs, yielding 288 outputs.These results support hardware- and implementation-aware grounding as a practical mechanism for calibrating what an EEG agent accepts, qualifies, or refuses; they do not establish clinical validity or a validated absolute cognitive-load index.

ru.title: Безопасная и надёжная ЭЭГ-аналитика через разделение детерминированного ядра и языковой модели
ru.desc: NeuraDock Agent — это открытая архитектура, которая объединяет детерминированный локальный движок обработки ЭЭГ с интерфейсом языковой модели для обеспечения точного анализа с учётом аппаратного обеспечения. Система разделяет численное ядро, которое обрабатывает записи, выполняет контроль качества и выполняет проверенные спектральные рабочие процессы, от слоя LLM, которому доступны только компактные и проверенные резюме. Контекстные пакеты модели содержат информацию об аппаратном обеспечении, реализованных алгоритмах, границах и научных ограничениях, тогда как сырые данные ЭЭГ остаются локальными для защиты приватности. Оценка показала детерминированность результатов, надёжность передачи данных и способность системы корректно обрабатывать граничные случаи благодаря информированности о возможностях оборудования.

en.title: NeuraDock Agent: Smart EEG Analysis with Local Data Security
en.desc: The NeuraDock Agent is a novel system that integrates a deterministic EEG processing engine with a language model interface to enhance the analysis of EEG data while ensuring data security. It addresses the challenges of using general language models by providing a hardware-aware context that informs the model about the specific capabilities and limitations of the EEG sensors and algorithms. The architecture allows for accurate parsing of EEG recordings, quality control, and the generation of machine-readable outputs, while keeping raw data local. Evaluation results demonstrate the system's reliability and boundary-awareness, confirming its potential for improving EEG data interpretation without compromising local data integrity.

zh.title: NeuraDock Agent：安全高效的脑电图分析工具
zh.desc: NeuraDock Agent 是一个结合了确定性脑电图（EEG）处理引擎和语言模型接口的开源架构，旨在确保准确的硬件感知分析，同时维护本地数据安全。该系统通过将本地 EEG 引擎与硬件感知语言层分离，能够有效解析记录、执行质量控制和生成机器可读的结果。我们对该系统进行了多层次的评估，结果显示在多次重复实验中，结构化结果保持一致，并且在不同的实验条件下，系统能够有效处理请求和故障注入。尽管这些结果支持了硬件和实现感知的基础，但并未确立临床有效性或验证绝对的认知负荷指数。
